prevalence.trend: increasing with diabetes prevalence and longer disease duration

incidence.value: 2-4% per year among people with diabetes

vulnerablePopulations: patients with limited access to foot care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical distal symmetric polyneuropathy assessment (symptoms + exam) with monofilament/vibration testing; rule out B12 deficiency (metformin), hypothyroidism, and alcohol-related neuropathy

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; annual foot screening recommended but inconsistently implemented
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; improved with glycemic control and structured foot care programs

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening with monofilament is inconsistent; referrals to endocrinology/neurology for complex cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Optimize glycemic control; foot care and ulcer prevention; neuropathic pain treatment (e.g., duloxetine, pregabalin, amitriptyline) and podiatry when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited podiatry/diabetic foot clinics and delayed ulcer care in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: APS chronic care platform and access to core diabetes medications in SUS
